findings.title: Critical Accessibility Compliance Gap
findings.description: LearnPath has significant WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility gaps affecting visually impaired and motor-disabled users, which poses a high risk of non-compliance with accessibility standards. This could lead to legal repercussions and loss of user trust.
assumptions: Accessibility compliance is essential for user trust and legal adherence.

evidence.excerpt: Implement ARIA roles and keyboard support for the course catalog and enrollment flow to enable navigation for visually impaired users.

recommendations: Conduct a full accessibility audit and implement necessary changes to meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards.

metadata.mitigation: Implement ARIA roles and keyboard support across the platform.

findings.title: High Risk of Non-Compliance with Accessibility Standards
findings.description: The current system has significant accessibility gaps that affect visually impaired and motor-disabled users, which poses a high risk of non-compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA standards. This could lead to legal repercussions and loss of user trust.
assumptions: Non-compliance with accessibility standards can lead to legal issues.

evidence.excerpt: Add closed captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls to the video player to enhance accessibility for deaf, hard-of-hearing, and motor-impaired users.

recommendations: Update the video player to include captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls.

metadata.mitigation: Update the video player to include necessary accessibility features.

findings.title: High Risk of Non-Compliance with Color Contrast Standards
findings.description: 60% of UI components fail to meet WCAG AA contrast ratios, making it difficult for users with visual impairments to read content effectively, which poses a high risk of non-compliance.
assumptions: Color contrast compliance is essential for accessibility.

evidence.excerpt: Revise UI components to meet WCAG AA contrast ratios across all text elements.

recommendations: Revise UI components to ensure compliance with WCAG AA contrast ratios.

metadata.mitigation: Revise UI components to meet contrast standards.

findings.title: Lack of VPAT for Compliance and Procurement
findings.description: The absence of a Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT) is a significant barrier for potential clients in government and education sectors, impacting purchasing decisions and compliance.
assumptions: A VPAT is essential for compliance and attracting government clients.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct a full accessibility audit, create a Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT), and publish an accessibility statement.

recommendations: Create and publish a VPAT to meet compliance requirements.

metadata.mitigation: Conduct a full accessibility audit and create a VPAT.

summary: Identified 4 risks (3 accessibility, 1 compliance). 4 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (5 blockers, 0 warnings).
